summary: Epic Not Fade Away jam, tight Dark Star > El Paso, Jerry shines
blurb: Features a monster Not Fade Away → Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad medley and a crisp Dark Star into El Paso segue. Jerry delivers soaring solos while Keith locks in the pocket; Phil’s thundering bass drives the band forward. Overall a high‑energy performance with tight transitions throughout.
review: On November 15, 1971 at Austin Municipal Auditorium the Dead served up one of those early‑’70s shows that still feels fresh. The first set builds momentum with a tight Dark Star that segues into an adventurous El Paso, then rockets forward into a blistering Not Fade Away → Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad jam that never lets up. Jerry’s guitar is on fire – shredding through Cumberland Blues, soaring over China Cat Sunflower, and laying down lyrical solos that lift the whole band. Keith Godchaux sits comfortably in the pocket, adding sparkling piano touches that especially shine on Ramble On Rose and during the El Paso interlude. Phil Lesh’s bass is thundering and audible, often locking horns with Bob in odd‑time moments, while Bill Kreutzmann provides a steady, propulsive beat that keeps the momentum flowing. The second set continues the high energy with strong renditions of Ramble On Rose, Cumberland Blues, and Sugar Magnolia, capped by the legendary Not Fade Away medley. Even with a few tape splices and minor vocal fuzz in the recordings, the musical performance is consistently praised across all sources – a true highlight of the 1971 tour.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings are described with positive language – 'monster', 'stellar', 'soaring solos', and consistent praise for the Not Fade Away medley, Dark Star > El Paso sequence, and band interplay. Even the few criticisms (blurry El Paso vocals) do not outweigh the overall enthusiasm, yielding a strong positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Provides the clearest mix with crystal‑clear jams, captures the monster Not Fade Away sequence and Dark Star > El Paso segue, and has only minor vocal fuzz and patched sections.

key_highlights: Monster Not Fade Away → Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad medley
Tight Dark Star into El Paso transition with soaring improvisation
Jerry's shredding on Cumberland Blues and soaring China Cat Sunflower solo
Keith’s piano locked in the pocket, adding sparkle throughout
song_highlights: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers shredding guitar on Cumberland Blues, lyrical phrasing on Ramble On Rose, and soaring solos that redefine the jam sections, especially during Not Fade Away and China Cat Sunflower.
band_performance.Phil: Provides thundering bass that drives the band forward, stands out in the mix, and even solos off Jerry during extended jams, adding depth to the low end.
band_performance.Bob: Offers strong rhythmic interplay with Jerry, solid vocal contributions, and occasional slide work; while opinions vary, his energy helps fuel the high‑octane moments.
band_performance.Billy: Keeps a steady, propulsive beat throughout; reviewers note his solid foundation, wishing only for a slightly louder mix.

must_listen_sequences: Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
